definitions.text: To move nearer to the point of perspective.
examples: She’ll be coming round the mountain when she comes the wrong way […]
I called the dog, but she wouldn't come.
I called the dog, but she wouldn't come.
Hold on, I'll come in a second.
You should ask the doctor to come to your house.
When a man uses a condom during sex, he takes all of his come with him, preventing her from getting pregnant.

definitions.text: Used to indicate a point in time at or after which a stated event or situation occurs.
examples: Leave it to settle for about three months and, come Christmas time, you'll have a delicious concoction to offer your guests.
Come retirement, their Social Security may turn out to be a lot less than they counted on.
Come now! Things could be worse.

definitions.text: An exclamation to express encouragement, or to precede a request.
examples: Come, come! You can do it.
Come now! It won't bite you.

definitions.text: Alternative form of comma in its medieval use as a middot ⟨·⟩ serving as a form of colon.
